name: I’m overwhelmed—what’s the very first thing I should do this week if I want to eventually get cited by AI?
description: Pick 10–20 “money questions” your ideal buyers ask AI assistants and build or refine one strong page per question. Put a direct answer at the top, use matching

mainEntity.name: I’m overwhelmed—what’s the very first thing I should do this week if I want to eventually get cited by AI?

acceptedAnswer.text:
Start by identifying the exact questions you want AI tools to associate with your brand, then build one page per question with answer-first content and clear structure.

You don’t need to fix your entire site at once; focus on the highest-impact queries where citing you would genuinely help buyers.

Key factors:
- Choose 10–20 “money questions” your ideal customer would ask an AI assistant.
- Create or refine pages so each one clearly answers a single core question.
- Use headings matching the question, plus a short direct answer at the top.
- Add FAQs covering realistic follow-ups, complications, and edge cases.

Once those pages are live, run citation audits across AI tools and compare your pages to cited ones.
Iterate based on what’s working rather than guessing in the dark.
acceptedAnswer.description: Pick 10–20 “money questions” your ideal buyers ask AI assistants and build or refine one strong page per question. Put a direct answer at the top, use matching headings and FAQs, then run AI queries to compare your pages to those already being cited and iterate from there.
